segments.text: And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar Nebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams, wherewith his spirit was troubled, and his sleep brake from him.
Then the king commanded to call the magicians, and the astrologers, and the sorcerers, and the Chaldeans, for to shew the king his dreams.
So they came and stood before the king.
I have dreamed a dream, and my spirit was troubled to know the dream.
segments.speaker: Nebuchadnezzar

segments.text: O king, live for ever: tell thy servants the dream, and we will shew the interpretation.
segments.speaker: Chaldeans

footnotes: O king…: (Chaldee, to the end of chapter seven)

segments.text: There is not a man upon the earth that can shew the king's matter: therefore there is no king, lord, nor ruler, that asked such things at any magician, or astrologer, or Chaldean.
segments.speaker: Chaldeans

segments.text: Then was the secret revealed unto Daniel in a night vision.
Blessed be the name of God for ever and ever: for wisdom and might are his:
segments.speaker: Daniel

segments.text: This is the dream; and we will tell the interpretation thereof before the king.
segments.speaker: Daniel

segments.text: Then the king Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face, and worshipped Daniel, and commanded that they should offer an oblation and sweet odours unto him.
